title: Understanding the Problem
text: The spider is hanging motionless, which means it is in equilibrium. The forces acting on it are balanced.

title: Identify the Forces
text: The only forces acting on the spider are its weight and the tension in the web strand.

title: Calculate the Weight
text: The weight of the spider can be calculated using the formula: weight $$=$$ mass * gravitational acceleration.

title: Calculate the Weight of the Spider
text: Use the formula weight $$=$$ mass * gravitational acceleration, where mass $$=$$ $$2\times{10}^{-5}$$ kg and gravitational acceleration $$=$$ $$9.8$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$.

title: Equilibrium Condition
text: Since the spider is in equilibrium, the tension in the strand is equal to the weight of the spider.

title: Determine the Tension
text: The tension in the strand is equal to the weight of the spider, which is $$1.96\times{10}^{-4}$$ N.
